title: Feature Metadata
description: JSON Schema for features' `metadata.yaml` files. This is a superset of the devcontainer-feature.json schema at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/devcontainers/spec/main/schemas/devContainerFeature.schema.json`, extended with internal-use-only properties (prefixed with an underscore) for richer metadata and documentation generation. All 'description' fields support simple (links, formatting) Markdown and HTML.

version.description: The version of the Feature. Follows the semanatic versioning (semver) specification.

keywords.description: List of strings relevant to a user that would search for this Feature.

options.description: Possible user-configurable options for this Feature. The selected options will be passed as environment variables when installing the Feature into the container. The names `prefix`, `prefix_discovery`, `prefix_symlinks`, `prefix_exports`, `prefix_activations`, and `runtime_path` — and any name matching `*_prefix`, `*_prefix_discovery`, `*_prefix_symlinks`, `*_prefix_exports`, or `*_activations` — are permanently reserved for code generation via `_prefix_groups` and must never be defined manually here.

propertyNames.description: Option keys must start with an ASCII letter and then contain only ASCII letters, digits, underscores, or hyphens.

method.description: Installation method option. Enum values must be drawn from the known set of installation method identifiers.

_options.description: Internal-use-only metadata for auto-generated options. The generator uses these metadata to dynamically generate the corresponding options in the `options` field and the associated installation script code.

user_file_fetch.description: Internal capability flag: feature accepts user-provided file/URI inputs and should receive shared fetch options.

installer_dir.description: When true, exposes installer_dir as a shared option and emits an INSTALLER_DIR auto-init snippet in the generated header.

gh_repo.description: The GitHub repository (or repositories) that this Feature installs. A plain `owner/repo` string for single-repo features; a named map for features that use multiple repositories, where each entry has a `tool_name` (display name) and a `slug` (`owner/repo`).

oneOf.description: Named map of GitHub repositories used by this Feature.

tool_name.description: Human-friendly name of the tool hosted in this repository.

_env_vars.description: Canonical path/filename values injected from metadata.shared.yaml. install_script.py emits one bash assignment per key, mapping snake_case `foo_bar` to `_FOO_BAR`.

_tool_name.description: The human-friendly name of the underlying tool or project that this Feature installs, used for display purposes in documentation and supporting tools.

platforms.description: List of supported platforms. If omitted, the Feature is assumed to support all platforms. Otherwise, every platform not matching at least one entry in this list is considered unsupported.

root.description: Whether the Feature requires root permissions to install. This can be either a boolean value (unconditional) or a list of platform match specifications that determine when root is required.

_prefix_groups.description:
Declares the installable-path groups that drive all of a feature's prefix, symlink, PATH-export, and activation machinery.

**Any feature that manages a user-configurable installation path must declare `_prefix_groups`.** The generator then emits all required user-facing options and bash helpers automatically. Manually defining `prefix`, `prefix_discovery`, `prefix_symlinks`, `prefix_exports`, `prefix_activations`, or `runtime_path` options — or any name matching `*_prefix`, `*_prefix_discovery`, `*_prefix_symlinks`, `*_prefix_exports`, or `*_activations` — is always forbidden; those names are reserved exclusively for this system.

For each declared group the generator produces:
- **User-facing options** (injected into `options` at build time): `{stem}_prefix`, `{stem}_prefix_discovery`, `{stem}_prefix_symlinks`, `{stem}_prefix_exports` (omitted when `symlink.skip`/`exports.skip` are true), and `{stem}_activations` (when `activation` is present). The bare names (`prefix`, `prefix_discovery`, etc.) are used for the unnamed group `""`.
- **Bash helpers** (rendered from templates into `install.bash`): `resolve_{id}_prefix()`, `create_{id}_symlinks()`, `export_{id}_path_main()`, and a `{stem}_activation_snippet()` stub (when `activation` is present). All are wired into `_prefix_post_install__generated()` automatically.

**Group key conventions:**
- Use `""` (empty string) as the key for a single, unnamed group.
- Use a non-empty slug (e.g. `"nvm"`) for each additional named group. The slug becomes both the option-name prefix and the function-name component (`resolve_nvm_dir()` if `option_name` overrides, otherwise `resolve_nvm_prefix()`, `create_nvm_symlinks()`, `export_nvm_path_main()`).

A feature may declare any number of groups.

additionalProperties.description: Configuration for one installable-path group. The group key (empty string for the default unnamed group, or a non-empty slug for named groups) determines the generated option names and bash function names.

option_name.description: Replaces the `{id}_prefix` stem for all generated options and bash helpers. Use only when the conventional name for the concept does not follow the `*_prefix` pattern. With `option_name: nvm_dir` on group `nvm`, the three generated options become `nvm_dir`, `nvm_dir_symlinks`, `nvm_dir_exports`, and the resolver is named `resolve_nvm_dir()`. Without this override, the group key `nvm` would produce `nvm_prefix`, `nvm_prefix_symlinks`, etc.

applies_when.description: Condition under which this group's generated options and bash helpers are active. When the condition does not match at install time, the helpers become no-ops.

prefix.description: Installation prefix configuration for this group.

root.description: Default prefix path when running as root (uid=0).

nonroot.description: Default prefix path when running as non-root.

bins.description: Binary filenames to symlink and/or export (e.g. `[gh]`, `[conda, mamba]`). Each binary is resolved at `${prefix}/{bin_dir}/{name}` and symlinked into `{symlink.root}/{name}`. Required unless both `symlink.skip` and `exports.skip` are true.

bin_dir.description: Subdirectory of PREFIX where the target binaries live. Determines the symlink source path (`${prefix}/{bin_dir}/{name}`) and the directory added to PATH by the exports helper (`${prefix}/{bin_dir}`).

description.description: Override the auto-generated description for the injected `{id}_prefix` option.

platform_overrides.description: Platform-specific prefix defaults evaluated before the uid-based root/nonroot fallback. Entries are tested in order; the first matching `when` condition wins. Uses `os__match_spec` at runtime. Supported keys: kernel, arch, id, id_like, codename.

items.description: A single platform override entry. Specify `when` plus at least one of `default`, `root`, or `nonroot`.

when.description: Condition object. All key=value pairs must match (AND logic). Case-insensitive. Supported keys: kernel, arch, id, id_like, codename.

default.description: Prefix to use when the condition matches, for both root and non-root. Overridden by `root` or `nonroot` when those are also specified.

root.description: Prefix to use when the condition matches and running as root (uid=0). Takes precedence over `default`.

nonroot.description: Prefix to use when the condition matches and running as non-root. Takes precedence over `default`.

symlink.description: Symlink creation configuration for this group. Controls the generated `{id}_prefix_symlinks` option and `create_{id}_symlinks()` helper.

root.description: Bin directory where symlinks are created when running as root. Each binary in `prefix.bins` lands at `{root}/{name}`, pointing to `${prefix}/{bin_dir}/{name}`. Decoupled from `prefix.root` — allows a non-standard install prefix to still expose binaries at a stable, well-known location.

nonroot.description: Bin directory where symlinks are created when running as non-root. Each binary in `prefix.bins` lands at `{nonroot}/{name}`.

skip.description: If true, do not inject the `{id}_prefix_symlinks` option and emit a no-op `create_{id}_symlinks()` stub.

description.description: Override the auto-generated description for the injected `{id}_prefix_symlinks` option.

exports.description: PATH-export configuration for this group. Controls the generated `{id}_prefix_exports` option and `export_{id}_path_main()` helper.

skip.description: If true, do not inject the `{id}_prefix_exports` option and emit a no-op `export_{id}_path_main()` stub.

description.description: Override the auto-generated description for the injected `{id}_prefix_exports` option.

activation.description: Shell activation snippet configuration for this group. When present, generates a `{stem}_activations` option and a `{stem}_activation_snippet()` stub, and emits a call to `shell__write_activation_snippets()` in `_prefix_post_install__generated()`. Body code overrides `{stem}_activation_snippet <shell>` to return the snippet content (stdout) and signal scope via exit code (0 = all contexts; 1 = interactive-only).

shells.description: Shell names to generate the `{stem}_activations` option for. Controls the option default.

description.description: Override the auto-generated description for the injected `{stem}_activations` option.

write_group.description: Write-permission group configuration for this prefix group. When present, generates `write_group` and `write_users` options and emits a `users__set_write_permissions` call in `_prefix_post_install__generated()`.

default.description: Default value for the injected `{stem}_write_group` option (the OS group name).

owner_var.description: Bash variable name whose value is passed as the owner argument to `users__set_write_permissions`. When omitted, `$(id -nu)` is used.

_dependencies.description: Build and runtime dependency manifests for the feature.

run.description: Runtime OS package dependencies, i.e., dependencies required for the feature to function after installation.

base.description: Runtime dependencies that are always required, regardless of input options. These are automatically installed in the autogenerated `install.bash` header.

additionalProperties.description: Runtime dependency groups that are conditionally required, based on input options. For each group, the autogenerated `install.bash` header will include a `_dep_install_runtime_<group>` function that installs the dependencies in that group; the feature's `install.bash` script can then call this function to install the dependencies when the relevant conditions are met.

build.description: Install-time OS package dependencies, i.e., dependencies required only during the installation of the feature, but not afterwards. These must only include the packages directly used by the `install.bash` script. Packages implicitly used through library function calls should not be added here.

base.description: Install-time dependencies that are always required, regardless of input options. These are automatically installed in the autogenerated `install.bash` header.

additionalProperties.description: Install-time dependency groups that are conditionally required, based on input options. For each group, the autogenerated `install.bash` header will include a `_dep_install_buildtime_<group>` function that installs the dependencies in that group; the feature's `install.bash` script can then call this function to install the dependencies when the relevant conditions are met.

_devcontainer.description: Example devcontainer.json configuration for this Feature, used to generate documentation and devcontainer.json files for live testing and demos.

dependsOn.description: An object of Feature dependencies that must be satisified before this Feature is installed. Elements follow the same semantics of the features object in devcontainer.json

installsAfter.description: Array of ID's of Features that should execute before this one. Allows control for feature authors on soft dependencies between different Features.

legacyIds.description: Array of old IDs used to publish this Feature. The property is useful for renaming a currently published Feature within a single namespace.

deprecated.description: Indicates that the Feature is deprecated, and will not receive any further updates/support. This property is intended to be used by the supporting tools for highlighting Feature deprecation.

capAdd.description: Passes docker capabilities to include when creating the dev container.

customizations.description: Tool-specific configuration. Each tool should use a JSON object subproperty with a unique name to group its customizations.

entrypoint.description: Entrypoint script that should fire at container start up.

init.description: Adds the tiny init process to the container (--init) when the Feature is used.

mounts.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

privileged.description: Sets privileged mode (--privileged) for the container.

securityOpt.description: Sets container security options to include when creating the container.

onCreateCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container. This command is run after "initializeCommand" and before "updateContentCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

updateContentCommand.description: A command to run when creating the container and rerun when the workspace content was updated while creating the container. This command is run after "onCreateCommand" and before "postCreateCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postCreateCommand.description: A command to run after creating the container. This command is run after "updateContentCommand" and before "postStartCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postStartCommand.description: A command to run after starting the container. This command is run after "postCreateCommand" and before "postAttachCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

postAttachCommand.description: A command to run when attaching to the container. This command is run after "postStartCommand". Each provided command will be run in parallel.

documentationURL.description: URL to documentation for the Feature.

licenseURL.description: URL to the license for the Feature.

_oci_ref.description: Internal-use-only OCI reference for this Feature

_lifecycle_key_prefix.description: Internal-use-only prefix for lifecycle command keys. With this prefix, the generator rewrites lifecycle command keys from `install` to `<prefix>--install`, etc. This allows multiple features to declare lifecycle commands without risk of key collisions, since each feature gets its own unique prefix.

id.description: ID of the Feature. The id should be unique in the context of the repository/published package where the feature exists and must match the name of the directory where the devcontainer-feature.json resides.

FeatureOption.description: Feature option. Two internal-use-only array types are added in addition to the standard boolean and string types for script generation purposes. Externally, these array types are represented as string options where array values are newline-delimited.

description.description: A description of the option displayed to the user by a supporting tool.

default.description: Default value if the user omits this option from their configuration.

enum.description: Allowed values for this option. Unlike 'proposals', the user cannot provide a custom value not included in the 'enum' array.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

proposals.description: Suggested values for this option. Unlike 'enum', the 'proposals' attribute indicates the installation script can handle arbitrary values provided by the user.

description.description: A description of the option value to display to the user by a supporting tool.

_applies_when.description: Condition under which this option is shown/active in supporting tools and considered applicable by the generator. When the condition does not match, the option is hidden and its value is ignored.

Mount.description: Mounts a volume or bind mount into the container.

type.description: Type of mount. Can be 'bind' or 'volume'.

LifecycleCommand.description: A set of commands to run at a specific lifecycle event. Write short per-task keys (e.g. ``install``, ``hello-world``); tooling expands them to ``<owner>-<repo>--<feature-id>--<task>`` when generating ``devcontainer-feature.json``. Legacy keys that already contain ``--<feature-id>--`` are rewritten to the same canonical form.

additionalProperties.description: A command entry with an executable command and a human-readable description of its purpose.

command.description: If this is a single string, it will be run in a shell. If this is an array of strings, it will be run as a single command without shell.

description.description: A short description of what this command does.

MethodValue.description: Known installation method values across DevFeats features. Features define their subset via their 'method' option's 'enum' array.

appliesWhen.description: Internal-use-only predicate indicating when an option or prefix group is applicable, based on the values of other options. Multiple conditions may be provided; they are OR-ed together (any one match is sufficient). Within a single condition object, all key=value pairs are AND-ed together.

items.description: A condition for when this option is applicable. This is an object where keys are option names and values are the required values for those options. All conditions in a single object are AND-ed together.

additionalProperties.description: The value of the other option that makes this option applicable. If an array of values is provided, values are OR-ed together.

MatchSpec.description: A platform specification for matching against the target system at runtime. A spec is a match when ALL variables match the target system (AND logic). Supports all variables from `/etc/os-release` (e.g., `NAME`, `VERSION_ID`, `VERSION`, `VERSION_CODENAME`, `ID`, `ID_LIKE`, etc.) plus `ARCH` (`uname -m`), `KERNEL` ('darwin', 'linux'), and `PM` ('apt', 'apk, 'dnf', 'pacman', 'zypper', 'brew', 'yum').

arch.description: CPU architecture from `uname -m`: x86_64, aarch64, armv7l, i686, etc.

id.description: OS ID from `/etc/os-release` (or 'macos' on macOS): ubuntu, debian, alpine, fedora, arch, rhel, opensuse-leap, macos, etc.

id_like.description: OS ID_LIKE from `/etc/os-release` (or 'macos' on macOS): debian, rhel, arch, suse, etc.
